title: Article Object Placement Doctrine
doctrine_basis: Approved visual/object placement must preserve article shape, justified text, reading width and mobile readability.

hero_image.preferred_location: near/top of article after heading/intro as already planned
hero_image.notes: Hero image may remain top-positioned; it must not disturb title, introduction, AG08G references or legacy governance blocks.

infographic.preferred_location: after relevant section paragraph, before next major section
infographic.notes: Infographic should support the argument, not dominate the article.

graph_or_chart.preferred_location: near the paragraph interpreting the data
graph_or_chart.notes: Charts must include caption, source note and accessible alt text.

table.preferred_location: after the explanatory paragraph introducing the data
table.width_behavior: responsive table wrapper required in future apply
table.notes: Tables with data must remain centrally aligned and must not compress body text.

figure_or_diagram.text_wrap: allowed only around object boundary if visually stable
figure_or_diagram.notes: Conceptual figures should preserve vertical flow and not split critical paragraphs.

map.preferred_location: only where geographic explanation is necessary
map.notes: Maps require stronger source/rights and accuracy review.

future_validation_requirements: Hero/image/figure/table must remain within article reading column.
Main article text remains justified after insertion.
No object overlaps references, footer or legacy governance blocks.
No horizontal overflow at mobile width.
Tables use responsive wrapper and are centrally aligned.
Text wrapping is allowed only where it preserves readability and object boundary integrity.
